title: Warm Spicy Jamaican Tiger Prawns Gratin
description: A spicy and flavorful Jamaican dish featuring tiger prawns, this gratin is a crowd-pleaser that is high in protein and can be served in individual mini cocottes. It is a great option for a weeknight dinner or special occasion.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat and saute the garlic and scotch bonnet peppers until fragrant.
Add the tiger prawns to the skillet and cook until they are pink and just cooked through.
In a separate saucepan, combine the heavy cream and jamaican jerk seasoning and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce the cream sauce by half and stir in the grated cheddar cheese until melted and smooth.
Butter four individual mini cocottes and divide the cooked prawns among them.
Pour the cream sauce over the prawns in each cocotte and top with additional grated cheese.
Bake the gratins in the preheated oven for 10 minutes, or until the cheese is golden and bubbly.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve immediately.
